findings.rule: Validate every external input; reject at the boundary
findings.message: Verified AC-3 holds at the real HTTP boundary, not just in the unit test: a global ValidationPipe({ whitelist: true, transform: true }) is wired in bootstrap, so @IsEnum(TaskPriority) rejects values like "urgent" before reaching the service. The spec test uses validateSync directly, which is acceptable given BE e2e specs are explicitly out of scope for this ticket.

findings.rule: Single shared TaskPriority type reused by model and DTO
findings.message: TaskPriority is declared once as a const-object + matching union and imported by both the DTO (@IsEnum runtime value) and the service. This satisfies the 'define once, reuse' scope item; full libs/shared-types extraction is correctly deferred per the ticket's out-of-scope list.

The ticket's in-scope list includes frontend changes (model, api service, task-list rendering/sort), but this diff delivers only the NestJS backend slice. All six acceptance criteria are backend service-level and are fully covered/verified here; the FE rendering has no associated AC. Flagging only so the FE slice is tracked in a separate change, not as a defect in this PR.

Ordering via PRIORITY_RANK with a decorate-by-index stable tiebreaker is correct and non-mutating (operates on a fresh array copy of the Map values). AC-4 and AC-5 verified by tests; insertion order is preserved for equal priorities.
